title: Python SDK with native snarkjs bridge for LangChain/CrewAI

artifacts.description: Package root exposing enroll_agent, prove_handshake, verify_handshake, delegate as top-level symbols
Node subprocess / WASM bridge that shells out to snarkjs, handles JSON I/O, manages process lifecycle, and raises typed exceptions on failure
Pydantic v2 models: EnrollmentResult, Handshake, ProofArtifact, DelegationToken — consumable directly by LangChain structured output and CrewAI task results
asyncio wrappers around bridge.py using asyncio.subprocess so async agent frameworks don't block the event loop
Synchronous wrappers using run_in_executor for frameworks that require blocking calls (e.g. CrewAI default executor)
BridgeError, CircuitNotFoundError, ProofVerificationError, SnarkjsNotFoundError — structured exceptions for framework error handling
Thin Node.js entry point that reads a JSON command from stdin, calls the appropriate snarkjs API (groth16.prove, groth16.verify, etc.), and writes a JSON result to stdout
Minimal Node package declaring snarkjs dependency, bundled into the PyPI wheel via hatch build hook
PEP 517 build config with hatch backend; declares pydantic>=2, optional langchain and crewai extras; includes node_modules bundling script
Unit tests for bridge.py using a mock Node subprocess; verifies JSON protocol, timeout handling, and error propagation
Pydantic model round-trip tests: serialization, deserialization, and schema compatibility with LangChain's with_structured_output
End-to-end integration tests requiring Node + snarkjs installed; tests enroll→prove→verify→delegate flow against real circuit artifacts
Tests that ProofArtifact and DelegationToken pass LangChain structured output parsing without schema errors
Tests that CrewAI Task output_pydantic=ProofArtifact works end-to-end with a mock crew
30-line getting-started guide showing pip install, Node prerequisite check, and a LangChain agent using prove_handshake
Full API reference for all four callables, Pydantic models, and exception hierarchy
JSON wire protocol spec for stdin/stdout communication between Python and Node bridge — versioned so future WASM backend can implement same interface

steps: Define the JSON wire protocol for the Python↔Node bridge: command envelope {op, params, circuit_path, zkey_path} and response envelope {ok, result, error}
Implement bolyra/node_bridge/index.js with handlers for ops: enroll, prove, verify, delegate — each calling snarkjs groth16 APIs and returning structured JSON
Write bolyra/bridge.py: spawn Node subprocess pointed at bundled index.js, write command JSON to stdin, read response JSON from stdout, enforce timeout, raise typed exceptions
Define Pydantic v2 models in bolyra/models.py for all proof artifacts; add model_json_schema() validation and LangChain-compatible field annotations
Implement bolyra/sync_api.py with enroll_agent(), prove_handshake(), verify_handshake(), delegate() as blocking callables using bridge.py
Implement bolyra/async_api.py with async variants using asyncio.create_subprocess_exec directly for non-blocking event loop integration
Write pyproject.toml with hatch build backend; add hatch_build.py hook that runs npm install inside bolyra/node_bridge/ and includes node_modules in the wheel
Add runtime Node.js detection in bolyra/__init__.py: check for node binary on PATH at import time, raise SnarkjsNotFoundError with install instructions if missing
Write unit tests for bridge.py mocking subprocess; write model round-trip tests; write LangChain and CrewAI compatibility tests
Write end-to-end integration test using real circuit .wasm and .zkey files from the existing @bolyra/sdk fixtures
Configure CI: matrix across Python 3.10/3.11/3.12, Node 18/20; run unit tests without Node, integration tests with Node
Write docs/quickstart.md and docs/api_reference.md; publish to PyPI as bolyra with version matching @bolyra/sdk TypeScript package
dependencies: existing circuit artifacts: .wasm and .zkey files from @bolyra/sdk
